reviews.review_description:
There's barely anything noteworthy to mention about the game. The gameplay consists of shooting NPC'S; something that many other games have done countless times before, and doing a much better job with it.

 Given that game has virtually no art style whatsoever as all models and UI are composed of a extremely overused asset pack found in so many other games, I was already tired of the design immediately after opening the game.

The game also has a severe lack of polish, with multiple bugs, glitches, and inconvenient features getting in the way of the most basic tasks such as turning or entering a level. I'm not shure if any testing went into this game prior.

While the game has a ever so slightly interesting concept of strategy, every conceivable aspect of it's execution is poor as there are no redeemable aspects of this title.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to leave a review.

I can understand that this early demo might not meet everyone's expectations — especially those with a keen developer eye. The current version focuses mostly on core systems and gameplay mechanics, and it's very much a work in progress.

That said, it's still in active development and built solo from the ground up, so feedback (even the tough kind) is always welcome. If you have specific bugs or improvement ideas, feel free to drop a message — would be happy to consider them as the project evolves.

Appreciate the honesty either way — wish you the best with your own projects too.
